title: (Cambodia) QC Associate Manager
Accountant
job_description:
In this hourly, remote contractor role, you will work as an Accounting Subject Matter Expert (SME) to review AI-generated accounting and finance responses and/or create expert accounting content, evaluating reasoning quality and step-by-step problem-solving while providing precise written feedback. You will assess solutions for accuracy, clarity, completeness, and adherence to the prompt; identify errors in bookkeeping logic, journal entries, accruals, reconciliations, financial statement presentation, and controls; fact-check technical accounting claims; write high-quality explanations and model solutions that demonstrate correct methods; and rate and compare multiple responses based on correctness and reasoning quality. This role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI Data Services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate, delivering training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation-model labs.
Important
There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.
Key Responsibilities
-  Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts and gold-standard accounting outputs (journal entries, explanations, close checklists, reconciliations logic, scenario analyses).
-  Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to improve correctness, clarity, and adherence to GAAP/IFRS and best practices.
-  Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI outputs for inaccuracies, misleading guidance, unsafe assumptions, and bias; validate reliability across business scenarios.
Your Profile
-  Bachelor’s degree (or higher) in Accounting, Finance, or a related field; CPA/ACCA/CMA (or local equivalent) is a strong plus.
-  5+ years of professional experience in accounting (public accounting, industry, shared services, or advisory).
-  Strong command of core accounting areas: revenue/expense recognition, accruals, prepaid/deferred items, fixed assets, inventory basics, payroll basics, and taxes awareness.
-  Proven ability to prepare and review journal entries, account reconciliations, and month-end close deliverables with attention to auditability.
-  Solid understanding of financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow) and common classification/presentation issues.
-  Familiarity with internal controls concepts (segregation of duties, approvals, documentation standards) and audit readiness.
-  Comfortable evaluating answers for internal consistency (sign conventions, debits/credits, timing, cut-off, supporting schedules) and business realism.
-  Minimum C1 English proficiency with the ability to write clear, structured explanations and follow detailed guidelines.
-  Reliable, self-directed, and able to deliver consistent quality in an hourly, remote contractor workflow across time zones.
-  Prior experience with AI data training/annotation, accounting QA, audit review, or technical accounting writing is strongly preferred.

title: Admission Manager
company: Agb Consulting-EoR, RPO, HR Outsources & Compliance
job_description:
ADMISSIONS MANAGER,
Report to: Director of Marketing & Admissions
An Admissions Manager will leads the end-to-end enrollment process, managing student recruitment, family communication, and application processing from initial inquiry to enrollment. The Admissions Manager acts as a key ambassador, overseeing the admissions team to meet targets, managing data via school information systems and ensuring compliance with educational regulations.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES & DUTIES:
• Admissions Strategy & Management: Oversee the entire admissions lifecycle, ensuring a seamless experience for families while implementing strategies to achieve enrollment goals.
• Customer Service & Communication: Serve as a primary point of contact, ensuring prompt (e.g., 24-hour) responses to inquiries, conducting school tours, and managing parent relations.
• Application Processing: Manage the processing of applications (Kindergarten-Grade 12), including coordinating student assessments/interviews and evaluating student files.
• Data Management & Reporting: Maintain accurate records in student information systems, generating daily/weekly reports on enrolment numbers, conversion rates, and sales targets for senior management.
• Team Leadership: Supervise and train admissions staff, coordinating work schedules to ensure coverage.
• Compliance & Administration: Ensure adherence to school recruitment policies, ministry of education regulations, and manage the re-enrolment/withdrawal processes.
• Marketing & Outreach: Collaborate with the marketing team to promote the school, attending fairs, and analyzing market conditions to update admissions materials.
QUALIFICATIONS & SKILLS REQUIRED:
• Experience: Previous experience in a supervisory role in an international school setting is often required.
• Communication: Excellent interpersonal skills for dealing with a diverse, international community.
• Technical Proficiency: Strong knowledge of school management software, and customer relationship management (CRM), and Microsoft Office Suite.
• Organization: Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple tasks and strict deadlines.
Safeguarding & Child Protection
The successful candidate is expected to always uphold the school’s safeguarding and child protection policies, demonstrate appropriate professional boundaries, and immediately report any concerns related to student welfare, safety, or wellbeing. All staff are required to complete safeguarding training and successfully pass all required background and reference checks before employment.

title: Advertising Expert
job_description:
In this hourly, remote contractor role, you will work as an Advertising Subject Matter Expert (SME) to review AI-generated advertising outputs and/or create expert ad copy and campaign assets, evaluating reasoning quality, strategy alignment, and step-by-step creative decision-making while providing precise written feedback. You will assess solutions for clarity, persuasion, brand fit, platform fit, and adherence to the prompt; identify issues in audience targeting logic, messaging hierarchy, offer framing, hooks/CTAs, compliance/claims risk, and creative-testing strategy; fact-check claims where needed; write high-quality explanations and model deliverables (ads, variations, briefs) that demonstrate best practices; and rate and compare multiple responses based on effectiveness, correctness, and reasoning quality. This role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI Data Services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate, delivering training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation-model labs.
Important
There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.
Key Responsibilities
-  Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts and gold-standard advertising outputs (ad copy sets, creative briefs, test plans, performance analyses) to guide AI learning.
-  Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to improve strategic fit, persuasion, clarity, and practical applicability.
-  Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI outputs for inaccuracies, misleading claims, policy violations, and bias; validate reliability across channels and audiences.
Your Profile
-  Bachelor’s degree (or higher) in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field (or equivalent professional experience).
-  5+ years of professional experience in advertising and/or performance marketing (agency or in-house), with hands-on campaign ownership.
-  Strong command of ad fundamentals: positioning, offer strategy, messaging frameworks, hooks, CTAs, and creative iteration.
-  Experience across major channels (as relevant): Meta (Facebook/Instagram), Google Ads/YouTube, TikTok, LinkedIn, programmatic, and/or connected TV.
-  Strong measurement instincts: CTR, CVR, CPA, ROAS, LTV, attribution limitations, and experiment design basics.
-  Comfort evaluating and improving ad copy for clarity, differentiation, compliance, and platform policy fit (especially in regulated categories).
-  Able to write gold-standard deliverables: ad variations, creative briefs, landing page critiques, and testing roadmaps—with clear rationale.
-  Minimum C1 English proficiency with excellent writing/editing skills.
-  Reliable, self-directed, and able to deliver consistent quality in an hourly, remote contractor workflow across time zones.
-  Prior experience with AI data training/annotation, creative QA, editorial review, or ad policy review is strongly preferred.

title: Area Manager
job_description:
Contexte et environnement
 
The oil products' market in Cambodia is relatively small but competition is fierce with a number of local and international players crowding the market. Convenience services and quality are becoming more and more popular and a major challenge for the Job Holder is thus to position Total brand within the market in such a way as to increase sales both within the store and on the fore court.
 
Activités
 
-  Defines and manages the retail strategy which cover all businesses in the stations by analyses the environment and the market in which operates to identify opportunities and threats, determine the SFS and non-fuel business opportunities and compliance with the Health/ Safety/ Environment, Safety of property and personal policy. 
-  Optimize profitability include franchise fee, rental, minimize loss/gain and other income from stations under the area of responsibilities. 
-  Fast track development of SFS businesses. 
-  Initiate the marketing and promotional strategy for all sale activities in conjunction with the Marketing department. 
-  Have a proper retail segmentation and renovation plan for all stations. 
-  Work together and propose to Optimization team to analyze profitability of the stations 
-  Create Financial Control and Forecasted P&L. 
-  Responsible for Top services, training need at the stations, analyzing overall sales and customer service performance in the territory and identifying potential for improvement at service stations, find out ways and means for improving sales. 
-  Visit and work with the Station Manager to ensure sales action plan are in place to address any areas of sales under performance. 
-  Ensure general upkeep of the service stations in terms of present-ability, safety and security. 
-  Supervise all service stations activities which include sales, customer relationships, stock and accounts. 
-  Immediately notify the supervisor in the event of any non-conformity or security related activities. 
-  Actively involving and promoting culture of food hygiene awareness and ensuring no food poison at stations under his/her territory Ensure highest and consistent service delivery by station staff in order to differentiate service offer from competitors 
-  Conduct competitor price survey and update competitors' activities/promotion in his/her trade area. 
-  Ensure all staff under his/her portfolio understand "How to" and implement active selling program. 
-  Ensure all staff are capable enough in doing his/her tasks well; especially, HESQ awareness, implement and share to others Monitor and update on time & Other duties assigned by Retail Manager. 
 
Profil du candidat
 
- Experience with B2B and B2C sale techniques
- Excellent people skills and an ability to build relationships and problem solving with people at all levels. Proven track record of building business and hitting sales targets.
- Strong management skill
- Good command of English.
-  Proficiency with Microsoft Office programs included PowerPoint, Word and Excel 
 
Informations supplémentaires
 
TotalEnergies valorise la diversité, promeut le développement individuel et offre des opportunités d'emploi égales à tous les candidats.

- Excellent organizational, analytical, and problem-solving abilities
- Strong communication and leadership skills for fostering a safety-first culture
- Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Science, Occupational Health, Safety Management, or a related field
- Experience in industrial or manufacturing environments is a plus

title: Assistant/Associate Merchandiser
job_description:
Job Summary
 JOB DESCRIPTION 
The Assistant/Associate Merchandiser is in local Supply Chain team for WSI and is responsible for interfacing with cross function team to optimize process flow to support objectives. They are the primary liaison among the vendors, Inventory team, transportation team, forwarders, managing order fulfilment, cargo delivery, vendor relations and key communications. This position includes situational analysis, strategy development, guidance and implementation for the functional disciplines of supply chain development.
Job Content And Duties
-  Order Management 
-  Monitor vendor PO fulfilment status by collecting response to pull up/push out/cancellation request from inventory team. 
-  Reduce back order by continuing to manage (push – chase) process with inventory. 
-  Drive vendors to support PO changes. 
-  Seasonal Floor set tracking for all brands and record for delay exceptions. 
-  Analyse PVR/PWR report to reduce long-time pending orders in system by vendor/inventory. 
-  Logistics Implement 
-  Work with cooperate transportation team to optimize the logistic strategy by updating the shipping instruction. 
-  Manage vendors to implement the most updated logistics policy to achieve our logistic strategy. 
-  Make sure the transportation in an efficiency way via good communication with related department. 
-  Build & Maintain the shipping guideline for new vendors to facilitate their On-boarding. 
-  On Time Delivery Management 
-  Monitor and improve vendor KPIs to increase vendor performance on cargo delivery. 
-  Track vendor on time booking performance to foresee any potential cause of the delay. 
-  Work with US transportation team to get accurate carrier space forecast, resolve the space issue in peak season. 
-  Drive Franchise shipment on time delivery with Merchant team bi-weekly basis to achieve target of each category. 
-  Manage the ocean/air forwarders to improve their service level on daily work. 
-  Troubleshoot any issues that come up on cargo delivery and work with vendors to avoid delays. 
-  Shipping Efficiency 
-  Measure container utilization for factory load or warehouse consolidation, to reduce light load ratio of the shipments. 
-  Communicate with vendor to fully understand our cargo consolidation requirement to reduce the co-load cost at destination. 
-  Expand the “Milk Run” loading to save the local charge for WSI/vendor by consolidating CFS cargos from different vendors. 
-  Implement logistics cost down & inventory control projects. 
-  SPO Delivery 
-  Ensure forwarder always keep high efficiency on international drop shipment. 
-  Monitor vendor to have cargo ready on time for SPO and follow the process in Citrix system. 
-  Support Merchant team to resolve any shipping issue, such as booking releasing, carrier space and so on. 
-  AD Hoc task and data analyzation 
-  Support cross function team for any project launch by shipping aspect. 
-  Generate and analyze the volume or OTD report per request from top management team. 
-  Develop the global connect system to all associates when necessary. 
Job Competencies & Experience
Knowledge
The incumbent who has knowledge of in the following areas are preferred:
-  Experience of PO management in sourcing industry. 
-  Knowledge of sourcing logistic mode and logistic cost management 
-  Knowledge of international trading, overall understanding export operation. 
Skills
The incumbent must demonstrate the following skills:
-  Fluent English oral and written skills, 2 nd foreign language Chinese is preferred. 
-  Excellent interpersonal skills 
-  Analytical and problem-solving skills 
-  Effective verbal and listening communications skills 
-  Attention to detail and high level of accuracy 
-  Effective written communications skills 
-  Computer skills including the spreadsheet and word-processing programs, and e-mail at a highly proficient level 
-  Ability to manage time well and handle difficult situations

title: Brand & Strategy Expert
job_description:
In this hourly, remote contractor role, you will work as a Brand & Strategy Subject Matter Expert (SME) to review AI-generated brand strategy, positioning, and go-to-market outputs and/or create expert brand and strategy content, evaluating reasoning quality and step-by-step strategic thinking while providing precise written feedback. You will assess solutions for accuracy, clarity, brand fit, and adherence to the prompt; identify issues in customer insight, segmentation, messaging hierarchy, positioning logic, differentiation, competitive analysis, and go-to-market planning; fact-check claims where needed; write high-quality explanations and model deliverables (e.g., positioning statements, messaging frameworks, briefs) that demonstrate correct strategic reasoning; and rate and compare multiple responses based on correctness, usefulness, and reasoning quality.
This role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI Data Services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate, delivering training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation-model labs. Your brand and strategy expertise directly helps improve the world’s premier AI models by making their strategic marketing outputs more rigorous, on-brand, actionable, and clearly explained.
Important
There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.
Key Responsibilities
-  Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts and gold-standard brand/strategy outputs (briefs, frameworks, messaging, GTM plans) to guide AI learning.
-  Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to improve strategic rigor, clarity, brand alignment, and practical usefulness.
-  Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI outputs for inaccuracies, hallucinated claims, bias, and brand-risk issues; validate reliability across industries and audiences.
Your Profile
-  Bachelor’s degree (or higher) in Marketing, Business, Communications, Psychology, or a related field (or equivalent professional experience).
-  5+ years of professional experience in brand strategy, product marketing, strategy consulting, or integrated marketing strategy (agency or in-house).
-  Strong command of positioning and messaging (value prop, proof points, objections, narrative, and tone adaptation).
-  Demonstrated ability to develop customer insights (qual/quant research interpretation, personas/JTBD, segmentation).
-  Strong strategic reasoning: translating ambiguous goals into clear strategy, prioritization, and measurable choices.
-  Ability to evaluate and improve deliverables such as brand briefs, messaging hierarchies, campaign concepts, creative strategy, and GTM plans.
-  Exceptional attention to detail when spotting weak logic, unsupported claims, misaligned tone, or risky assertions; minimum C1 English proficiency.
-  Reliable, self-directed, and able to deliver consistent quality in an hourly, remote contractor workflow across time zones.
-  Previous experience with AI data training/annotation, editorial QA, strategy ops, or content review workflows is strongly preferred.

title: Client Services Executive
job_description:

 U.N.I.X is a Cambodia-based creative company specializing in advertising, influencer marketing, video production, and studio rentals. Through its four divisions—Unix Creative, Show Plus, Unix Production, and Cinespace Studio—the company delivers integrated solutions spanning marketing strategy, digital content, and production services. Established in 2019, Unix Production brings strong expertise in filmmaking and branded content, while Cinespace Studio provides professional studio spaces and equipment. With a passionate and innovative team, U.N.I.X focuses on exceptional storytelling, strategic thinking, and high-quality execution to help brands grow and succeed.
Role Description
 The Client Services Executive is a full-time, on-site role based in Phnom Penh, Cambodia. This role is responsible for managing day-to-day client communication, understanding client objectives, and ensuring projects are delivered on time, on brief, and within budget. The Client Services Executive will collaborate closely with internal teams across creative, production, and influencer marketing to coordinate campaigns, prepare proposals, and support pitches. Daily tasks include maintaining accurate client records, tracking project performance, gathering feedback, and proactively identifying opportunities to add value. The role also involves monitoring campaign results, preparing client reports, and contributing to continuous improvement in service quality and client satisfaction.
Qualifications

title: Freelance Market Research Analyst - Cambodia
job_description:
Founded in 1972, Euromonitor International is the world's leading provider of global business intelligence and strategic market analysis, with a presence spanning 16 countries and regions. Our dynamic team of over 1,000 in-country analysts worldwide empowers us to deliver exceptional, trustworthy business insights across more than 100 countries.
We equip our clients with the knowledge they need to navigate market dynamics, decode consumer trends, analyze competitive landscapes and uncover exciting growth opportunities across a diverse array of industries and markets.
Our research covers a wide range of topics, including:
- Market size and share analysis
- Competitive benchmarking
- Go-to-market opportunities
- Consumer and channel insights
- Strategic positioning analysis
We are on the lookout for detail-oriented and commercially savvy analysts to contribute to regional and international research projects on freelance, project-based engagements. We value long-term partnerships and welcome ongoing collaboration with exceptional performers.
Scope:
- Conduct secondary research into industries, companies, consumer trends and more.
- Execute store visits and retail audits to gather brand and product information.
- Carry out cold calls and trade interviews with companies and associations to extract qualitative and quantitative insights across various industries.

- Prepare client-ready research deliverables, including written analyses, presentations, databases and reports.

Requirements
- Professional fluency in English to ensure clear and effective written and verbal business communication - local language proficiency would be an added advantage.
- Exceptional numeracy skills with the ability to interpret, cross-reference and analyze data from diverse sources.
- Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, etc.) to support project tasks.
- Ability to work independently to consistently meet deadlines in a remote working environment.
- Prior experience in market research or consulting is highly desirable with exposure to different research methodologies such as:
- - Trade interviews - demonstrated experience in conducting cold calls would be an added advantage
- - Store visits/audits/checks
- - Report writing
- - Desk research

planning and inventory control.

title: Medium Business Loan Officer - Level 1
job_description:
Job Purpose
To acquire and onboard medium business loan clients as well as analyze and process their loan application by conducting credit investigations, scoring, credit checking, and other underwriting activities in order to ensure good lending and loan quality of Medium Business Loan of the Branch.
Location
 KRONG KAMPONG SOAM BRANCH - SANGKAT BET TRANG 
Major Areas of Responsibility
- Maintain existing and build new medium business clients in accordance with the guidelines stated in the medium business loan policies and procedures to ensure the secured loans.
- Collect all clients’ required documents for loan application to verify their eligibility for the loan.
- Gather valid applicants’ financial records by obtaining and compiling copies of clients’ credit histories, medium business financial statement, and other financial information in order to prevent from being conned.
- Conduct credit investigation to analyze applicants’ financial status, credit, property evaluations, repayment capacity to determine feasibility of granting loans.
- Conduct a site visit during loan assessment process to obtain in-depth information concerning to the purpose of customer’s loan application and ensure sufficient and acceptable customer security for the loan.
- Process and prepare Credit Appraisal Report including detailed loan appraisal writeup and detailed financial analysis of the clients in order to submit to higher authority for loan approval.
- Explain to clients the different types of loans and credit options that are available as well as the terms of those services for further cross-selling.
- Make regular site visits to existing customers to determine whether the loan should stay the same or be upgraded/downgraded due to the actual condition of their loan collateral and/or financial situation as well as to develop relationship with customers for further cross-selling.
- Closely monitor on recommended/processed loan for timely repayment and to ensure Portfolio At Risk remains within acceptable level.
- Work closely with Head Office to follow up on all post disbursement conditions and financial covenants and ensure full compliance.
- Follow up delinquent client immediately and refer such case to the supervisor to proactively handle the branch loan portfolio. 
- Discuss credit-related issues and loan packaging to provide sound financial advices and tailored solutions for the clients as well as to take the opportunity to cross-sell other banking products.
- Handle clients’ issues or complaints to find an amicable solution for all and process to higher authority if needed.
- Stay abreast of new types of loans and other financial services and products to better meet customer needs.
Qualifications
- Bachelor’s degree in finance and banking, accounting, or any relevant field.
- Minimum one year of working experiences in banking and financial services industry.
- Good knowledge of the bank branch operations, rules and regulations, laws, granting loans, and credit risk management.
- Demonstration of presentation and sales skills.
- Good customer service, client relationship management, communication, negotiation, problem solving and interpersonal skills.
- Ability to use and communicate in both Khmer and English.
- Good Microsoft Office and Bank’s software.
- Good math skills and ability to spot numerical errors.
- Good problem solving, negotiation and interpersonal skill.
- Strong dedication to accuracy and efficiency.

title: National Intern for People and Culture (HR), Phnom Penh, Cambodia
job_description:
UNICEF works in some of the world’s toughest places, to reach the world’s most disadvantaged children. To save their lives. To defend their rights. To help them fulfill their potential.
Across 190 countries and territories, we work for every child, everywhere, every day, to build a better world for everyone.
And we never give up.
For every child, 
commitment
UNICEF started operation in Cambodia in 1952 and opened first country office in 1973 in Phnom Penh. More information on what we do in Cambodia is available at 
https://www.unicef.org/cambodia/
.
How can you make a difference?
UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to promote the rights of every child. In Cambodia, the Country Programme 2024–2028 supports national efforts to ensure that all children, especially the most disadvantaged, realize their rights to survival, development, protection, and participation.
The People and Culture (P&C) Team play a critical role in enabling UNICEF to deliver on its mandate by fostering a supportive workplace, strengthening organizational culture, and ensuring effective people management practices.
To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have…
- Currently enrolled in at least the 3rd year or recently graduated in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field
- Proficiency in English and Khmer (written and spoken)
- Strong organizational and communication skills
- Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools and Canva
- Interest in people management, workplace culture, and organizational development
- Commitment to UNICEF’s mission and values. 
The Intern Should Be
- Respect for diversity and inclusion
- Sensitivity to child rights and safeguarding principles
- Ability to work in a multicultural environment
- Integrity and respect for confidentiality 
Duration And Working Hours
The internship is full-time, 4 months between July- October, from Monday to Friday (8 hours per day). Flexible working hours may be arranged in consultation with and upon approval of the supervisor.
Learning And Professional Development Opportunities
The intern will have the opportunity to:
- Gain practical experience in People and Culture within a UN organization
- Learn about recruitment, staff engagement, wellbeing, and organizational culture
- Receive coaching and feedback from experienced P&C professionals
- Develop professional and interpersonal skills
Benefits Of Internship
- Monthly stipend: USD 300 (if not otherwise funded)
- One-time reimbursement (e.g. insurance): up to USD 100
- UNICEF covers official duty travel if required
- Laptops and office space and facilities provided
- Reasonable accommodation for people with disabilities
- Flexible working arrangements.
Please find the terms of reference for this assignment in the attachment below.
ToR_National Intern P&C - TMS.docx
For every Child, you demonstrate...
UNICEF’s Core Values of Care, Respect, Integrity, Trust, Accountability and Sustainability (CRITAS) underpin everything we do and how we do it. Get acquainted with Our Values Charter: UNICEF Values
UNICEF Competencies Required For This Post Are...
-  Builds and maintains partnerships (2) Demonstrates self-awareness and ethical awareness (3) Drive to achieve results for impact (4) Innovates and embraces change (5) Manages ambiguity and complexity (6) Thinks and acts strategically (7) Works collaboratively with others.
During the recruitment process, we test candidates following the competency framework. Familiarize yourself with our competency framework and its different levels: competency framework here.
UNICEF is here to serve the world’s most disadvantaged children and our global workforce must reflect the diversity of those children. The UNICEF family is committed to include everyone, irrespective of their race/ethnicity, age, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, socio-economic background, or any other personal characteristic.
We offer a wide range of benefits to our staff, including paid parental leave, breastfeeding breaks and reasonable accommodation for persons with disabilities. UNICEF strongly encourages the use of flexible working arrangements.
UNICEF has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNICEF, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination. UNICEF is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children. All selected candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous reference and background checks, and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. Background checks will include the verification of academic credential(s) and employment history. Selected candidates may be required to provide additional information to conduct a background check.
Remarks
UNICEF appointments are subject to medical clearance. Issuance of a visa by the host country of the duty station, which will be facilitated by UNICEF, is required for IP positions. Appointments may also be subject to inoculation (vaccination) requirements, including against SARS-CoV-2 (Covid). Government employees that are considered for employment with UNICEF are normally required to resign from their government before taking up an assignment with UNICEF. UNICEF reserves the right to withdraw an offer of appointment, without compensation, if a visa or medical clearance is not obtained, or necessary inoculation requirements are not met, within a reasonable period for any reason.
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and advance to the next stage of the selection process.

title: Officer, Procurement & Logistics
job_description:
Job Summary
 
The overall role of Officer, Procurement & Logistics is to manage purchasing activities, supplier coordination, shipment arrangements, and inventory monitoring by ensuring timely procurement of materials and efficient logistics management while maintaining cost control and compliance with company requirements.
Key Responsibilities 
 
1      Source and evaluate suppliers for packaging materials, logistics services, and other operational requirements to ensure quality, cost efficiency, and reliable supply continuity.
2      Request quotations, compare pricing, and negotiate commercial terms with suppliers to achieve competitive purchasing conditions and cost savings for the company.
3      Coordinate import/export shipments with suppliers, freight forwarders, and related parties to ensure smooth and timely transportation of goods.
4      Verify shipping documents such as Commercial Invoice, Packing List, Bill of Lading, and other logistics documents to ensure accuracy and compliance with shipping requirements.
5      Track shipment schedules and monitor delivery status to ensure on-time arrival of goods and minimize delays.
6      Monitor logistics costs and optimize transportation efficiency to support cost control and operational effectiveness.
7      Maintain accurate logistics and shipment records in order to prepare shipment summaries, logistics reports, and support internal tracking purposes.
8      Ensure compliance with company procedures, import/export regulations, and shipping requirements to minimize operational risks and maintain smooth logistics operations.
Success Factors
1.     Materials and logistics services are sourced on time, at competitive cost, and with reliable quality.
2.     Shipments and deliveries are coordinated smoothly with minimal delays or issues.
3.     Shipping documents and records are accurate, complete, and submitted on time.
4.     Strong coordination is maintained with suppliers, forwarders, and internal departments to support smooth operations.
Qualifications 
 
1.     Education: 
Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business Administration, or related field.
 
2.     Experiences: 
o  Minimum 1–3 years of experience in procurement, logistics, or supply chain operations.
o  Experience in import/export procedures is preferred.
3.     Skills and knowledge: 
o  Good negotiation and communication skills.
o  Strong analytical and problem-solving ability.
o  Proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel.

title: Pharmacist
job_description:
In this hourly, remote contractor role, you will work as a Pharmacist Subject Matter Expert (SME) to review AI-generated medication- and pharmacy-related responses and/or generate expert pharmacy content, evaluating reasoning quality and step-by-step clinical problem-solving while providing precise written feedback. You will assess solutions for accuracy, clarity, safety, and adherence to the prompt; identify errors in pharmacotherapy reasoning, dosing logic, contraindications, interactions, monitoring, and patient counseling; fact-check medical and drug information; write high-quality explanations and model solutions that demonstrate correct clinical reasoning; and rate and compare multiple responses based on correctness and reasoning quality. This role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI Data Services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate, delivering training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation-model labs. Your pharmacy expertise directly helps improve the world’s premier AI models by making their medication guidance, clinical safety reasoning, and patient-facing explanations more accurate, reliable, and clearly explained.
Important
There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.
Key Responsibilities
-  Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts and gold-standard pharmacy responses across therapeutics, drug information, counseling, and medication safety topics.
-  Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to improve safety, clinical correctness, clarity, and contextual relevance.
-  Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI outputs for inaccuracies, unsafe recommendations, and bias; validate reliability across use cases and patient populations.
Your Profile
-  PharmD or Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Pharmacy (BPharm/MPharm) and active pharmacist licensure (or eligibility), with strong grounding in pharmacology, therapeutics, and medication safety.
-  5+ years of professional experience in pharmacy practice (community, hospital, ambulatory care, clinical pharmacy, managed care, or similar).
-  Confident in pharmacotherapy reasoning (indication-based selection, dosing, titration, monitoring, deprescribing) and explaining why a recommendation follows from the evidence.
-  Strong understanding of disease states, guideline-based therapy, medication reconciliation, and patient care workflows across settings (ED/inpatient/outpatient).
-  Exceptional attention to detail when fact-checking drug content and identifying unsafe assumptions, missing contraindications, interaction risks, allergy considerations, or misinterpretation of labs; minimum C1 English proficiency.
-  Comfortable evaluating answers for internal consistency (timelines, physiology, dosing units, formulation selection), appropriateness for setting, and patient safety implications.
-  Reliable, self-directed, and able to deliver consistent quality in an hourly, remote contractor workflow across time zones.
-  Previous experience with AI data training/annotation, medication-use evaluation, drug information, clinical documentation review, PBM/managed care review, or healthcare editorial QA is strongly preferred.

title: Quality Control Inspector (Softline)
job_description:
Company Description
At QIMA, we’re on a mission to help our clients make products consumers can trust.
Working with over 30,000 global brands, retailers, manufacturers and food growers, we are on the ground wherever products are made. We help businesses secure every step of their supply chain with quality inspections, supplier audits, certifications, and lab testing, all powered by our intelligent digital platform.
Our team of 5,000 QIMates (and counting) across 40 offices and laboratories, are united in our shared passion for innovation and integrity, and guided by our QIMA values in the decisions we make every day. We believe in the work we do, and in making a positive difference in the world. Does this sound like something you’d like to be a part of?
Our Consumer Goods Division is dedicated to helping brands, retailers and manufacturers worldwide deliver safe, quality and ethically made products to their consumers. Every day at work, our inspectors, auditors, and lab techs use their expertise to help safeguard the products you use every day in your home: from clothes and footwear to toys and baby products, to electronics, appliances, and furniture.
Our operations department’s purpose is to deliver our industry-leading services to our clients while ensuring that all the work we do is performed to the QIMA standard of quality
Job Description

Your main duties will revolve around on-site quality inspections of our clients’ products.
- Check email daily to receive inspection documents.
- Attend to inspections on time and communicate with supplier in a professional way.

- Fulfill inspection reports according with company procedures and timelines.

- Possess a college degree or above in apparel or related fields
- Experience of more than 3 years in quality management for garment/textile products

- Good command of English (spoken and written)
- Great attention to detail and strong professional ethics

title: Senior Manager, Public Relations

- Monitor public perception and media sentiment regarding the bank.
